Up and Running in 60 Seconds.

The Claude Code Skills Bundle gives you 50 pre-built skills that handle real developer work. Structured systems that produce polished, ready-to-use output every time.

1

Download

Grab the skills folder and drop it into ~/.claude/skills/. Setup guide included.

2

Open Claude Code

Run claude in any project. Skills are ready immediately, no config required.

3

Invoke

Type /skill-name in Claude Code. Or just describe what you need. Claude detects the right skill.

If you write code and want AI that actually keeps up, this bundle was made for you.

Solo Developers

Freelance Developers

50 Skills. Every Part of Your Codebase.

Each skill asks the right questions and delivers polished, ready-to-use output. Here's a taste.

├──code-quality/code-review, security-review, performance-review, refactor, clean-code …

├──documentation/generate-docs, readme-write, api-docs, changelog, explain-code …

└──productivity/onboard-codebase, task-breakdown, sprint-plan, standup
Get the Bundle · $15
claude@skills ~ % cat basics.md

What Are Claude Code Skills?

Skills are structured instruction files that extend what Claude Code can do. Each skill contains expert-level frameworks, step-by-step processes, and output templates, so Claude delivers polished, professional results instead of generic AI output.

> Not prompts. A prompt is a one-off question. A skill is a structured system that guides Claude through a multi-step process, asking the right questions, applying proven frameworks, and formatting output professionally.

> Built on the official standard. These skills follow Anthropic's official SKILL.md format (the same spec used internally). Each skill is a simple folder with a SKILL.md file. No proprietary lock-in, no complicated setup.

> Works in every project. Drop the skills folder into ~/.claude/skills/ and they're available across all your projects. Invoke with /skill-name or let Claude detect when to use them automatically.

claude@skills ~ % man claude-skills

Got Questions?

A skill is a structured SKILL.md file that extends what Claude Code can do. Each one contains expert-level instructions, proven frameworks, and output templates. When you install a skill, Claude automatically knows when to use it, or you can invoke it directly with a /slash-command. Think of them as hiring a specialist, not copying a prompt.
You need basic terminal comfort: opening Terminal and running a command. The skills work in any codebase. Many of the reference skills also work as learning tools: ask Claude to explain what it found and why.
After purchase you get a download link with all 50 skill folders and a step-by-step guide. One command: cp -r skills/ ~/.claude/skills/ and they're live in every project. Takes about 60 seconds. No coding or config required.
No. Each skill is a structured system built on Anthropic's official SKILL.md format, not a prompt you paste. Skills guide Claude through multi-step processes, apply established frameworks (OWASP for security, AAA for tests, Conventional Commits for git), and format output professionally. They're closer to hiring a specialist than copying a prompt.
All skills are language-agnostic. Code examples use TypeScript/JavaScript, Python, and SQL where relevant, but the principles and patterns apply to any stack: React, Node.js, Django, Rails, Go, whatever you're building in.
You're covered by a 30-day money-back guarantee. Try every skill in the bundle. If they don't save you real time and effort, email us for a full refund. No hoops, no questions.
